item.title: UK tightens investment trust rules to close gaps exposed by Saba Capital
item.description: Watchdog’s proposals may make it harder for activist investors to control listed closed-end companies

item.title: EU plans 15% tax on aluminium scrap exports
item.description: Move by Brussels aims to protect domestic producers and prevent valuable metal heading to US and Asia

item.title: Germany urges EU to suspend methane rules after US pressure
item.description: Energy minister warns of risk to gas supplies if planned rules affecting imports come into force

item.title: Parisians seek refuge from heatwave in air-conditioned hotels
item.description: People booking rooms during the day to work while others seek night-time respite from stifling apartments

item.title: Unilever explores bid for $4bn supplement maker Thorne
item.description: Dove soap maker seeks to boost health and wellness portfolio after spinning off food business

item.title: Your summer holiday is a retirement killer
item.description: The capitalised value of that all-inclusive fortnight will scare you

item.title: Inheritance tax changes on UK pensions fuel surge in demand for annuities
item.description: Increased rates add to attractiveness of regular income

item.title: Brussels launches antitrust probe into French drugmaker Sanofi
item.description: Competition watchdog examining whether group implemented ‘misleading’ campaign against rival flu vaccine maker

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Rwanda gold refinery
item.description: Operation accused over smuggling from mineral-rich DR Congo

item.title: UK government to subsidise bank loans for green home improvements
item.description: Scheme aims to boost take-up by backing up to 20 per cent of loans for installation

item.title: German financial regulator probes Zalando over €1.1bn deal disclosure
item.description: BaFin says fashion retailer may have failed to provide information on a transaction linked to About You acquisition

item.title: Britain backs electricity storage projects as heatwave strains grid
item.description: Provisional approval of battery and hydropower projects comes after high temperatures reduce gas and solar generation

item.title: Volkswagen to axe up to 100,000 jobs in sweeping cost-cutting drive
item.description: Restructuring would remove close to one in six workers and rank among biggest corporate lay-offs of all time

item.title: Heathrow warns on global travel demand in wake of Iran conflict
item.description: UK airport lowers passenger outlook as Middle East volatility weighs on traffic volumes

item.title: The leadership change in US stocks
item.description: A market seeking direction

item.title: DeepSeek plans hiring spree in escalation of China’s AI talent war
item.description: Advertised roles suggest company focused on commercialising frontier research

item.title: Global markets fall as investors fret about AI demand
item.description: Apple’s price rises and fears over delay to OpenAI’s IPO send Asian bourses sharply lower

item.title: FirstFT: Wall Street buys Warsh’s tough talk
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: how the DeepMind mafia brought the AI boom to London

item.title: Rheinmetall gambled on Germany’s doomed warship project — and lost
item.description: Arms company’s €15bn frigate blow tests faith among investors

item.title: Binance to stop providing services to EU clients after failing to obtain licence
item.description: World’s biggest crypto exchange tells customers how to withdraw their money as MiCA rules set to come into force

item.title: Trump son set for Kalshi windfall after prediction platform gave him stake
item.description: Privately owned company’s valuation has soared as US administration has adopted a light-touch approach to the sector

item.title: How JPMorgan became Jamie Dimon
item.description: The 70-year-old is one of the longest-serving bosses in finance

item.title: How the DeepMind mafia brought the AI boom to London
item.description: The tech sector is buzzing in Britain. But can it ever be more than a US outpost?

item.title: Swatch seeks $170mn from Samsung over digital clones of its watches
item.description: Swiss watchmaker accuses technology group of ‘large-scale appropriation’ of luxury designs in London lawsuit

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX plans Starlink mobile push into US consumer market
item.description: Move would test whether group can turn sky-high ambition into a mass-market phone business

item.title: Revolut scraps ‘remote-first’ working for graduate hires
item.description: Europe’s most valuable fintech will require junior staff to spend at least three days a week in the office from next year

item.title: Zambia’s state-owned miner seeks to revive London listing
item.description: ZCCM chief executive hopes to publish 2025 results and resume UK trading “maybe within two months”

item.title: Chinese carmakers ‘knocking at US gate’ from Mexico and Canada
item.description: Beijing-subsidised vehicles emerge as flashpoint in North American trade relationship

item.title: Companies rush to fire high earners before UK ends cap on unfair dismissal payouts
item.description: Labour government is removing £123,543 limit on compensation as part of package to boost workers’ rights

item.title: The AI factory: the rewiring of India's tech industry
item.description: Outsourced data services may not be enough for India to thrive in the AI era
